other_observers: Gary Mathews, Judy Wright & Maggie Boswell all saw the bird on the 6th; I originally found the bird by its vocalizing on the 5th and tracked the call down until I saw the bird
duration: 45 minutes on the 5th and 30 minutes on the 6th
specific_location: Blue Cloud Farm. There is a small pond just to the north of the barn through a pasture and the bird was on the west end of that pond
description: The bird was identical to the one in the Sibley guide  I have a few photos that are submittedBehaviors: It moved very rapidly through the Cottonwood Trees. The bird made short flights staying primarily within three feet of the ground. As I got close to the bird it's tail would rise before it flew to another perch. I did see it on a number of occasions gleaning insects from the branches.Call: It began with a few sharp chips then a rapid series of sticatto chips. The call was identical to the Recorded call (Stokes eastern I believe) that was played back.Plumage: Breeding
